perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Recipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to any party it deems necessary, provided that the Recipient believes that those parties will keep the information confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition required employees, agents, consultants, and contractors to have written agreements. Now, the recipient can disclose to any party, removing the obligation for written agreements. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear if Recipient is obligated to have a written agreement with the 3rd party, which contradicts the original agreement, creating an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient ag rees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential and to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information with the same degree of care with which it protects the confidentiality of its own confidential information, but i n no event with less than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to any party it deems necessary, provided that the Recipient believes that those parties will keep the information confidential. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purp ose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires written agreements for employees, agents, consultants, and contractors to whom Confidential Information is disclosed. The changed text allows disclosure to 'any party it deems necessary' as long as the Recipient believes they will keep the information confidential, removing the requirement for a written agreement. The contradicted text mentions the Recipient's ability to disclose to any party it deems necessary if the recipient believes the party will keep the information confidential, which is now present in both the changed text and contradicted text. This change creates a direct conflict because the original text outlines specific parties and requires written agreements. The changed text broadens the scope to 'any party' without requiring written agreements, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information, but is not obligated to return or destroy any derivative works or analyses created using that information.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction. The original text stipulates the return of all confidential information or certification of its destruction. The modified text allows the Recipient to retain derivative works, creating ambiguity about what constitutes 'Confidential Information' that must be returned and undermining the purpose of the NDA. It directly contradicts the provision requiring return of 'all' Confidential Information in the event of the request, resulting in uncertainty in enforcement of the initial provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidenti al Information shall remain with Disclosing Party or its licensors. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Recipient to return or destroy *all* Confidential Information. The changed text introduces an exception by allowing the Recipient to retain derivative works. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that "All right title and interest in and to the Confidenti al Information shall remain with Disclosing Party". Because the changed text allows the recipient to retain derivative works, it contradicts the notion that all right and title remains with the Disclosing Party, constituting a strong in-text contradiction as per the provided explanation, and the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.changed_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED WITH A WARRANTY OF ACCURACY TO THE BEST OF THE DISCLOSING PARTY'S KNOWLEDGE. THE DISCLOSING PARTY SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INACCURACIES, BUT WILL STRIVE TO PROVIDE CORRECT INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction to the 'AS IS' clause. Now, the Disclosing Party provides a warranty of accuracy, while still stating they aren't liable for inaccuracies. This creates uncertainty in enforcement because the recipient may expect some level of accuracy, directly undermining the original disclaimer. It will create In-Text Contradiction with the original clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right title and interest in and to the Confidenti al Information shall remain with Disclosing Party or its licensors. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED WITH A WARRANTY OF ACCURACY TO THE BEST OF THE DISCLOSING PARTY'S KNOWLEDGE. THE DISCLOSING PARTY SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INACCURACIES, BUT WILL STRIVE TO PROVIDE CORRECT INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the confidential information is provided 'AS IS' with no warranties regarding accuracy. The changed text introduces a 'warranty of accuracy,' directly contradicting the original 'AS IS' provision. This creates an in-text contradiction because the recipient now has an expectation of accuracy, while the disclosing party attempts to limit liability. This conflicts with the intention of the 'AS IS' clause found in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confident ial Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain, other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient; or (b) at any time rightfully received from a third party which had the right to and transmits it to the Rec ipient without any obligation of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confident ial Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain; or (b) at any time received from a third party, regardless of whether the third party had the right to disclose it.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the 'other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient' clause in (a) and the 'rightfully received' and 'obligation of confidentiality' clauses in (b). Now, if the Recipient causes information to enter the public domain, or receives confidential info from a third party *without* the right to disclose, it's no longer considered confidential under the agreement. This creates a contradiction because the Recipient is not accountable for breaches or protecting info obtained unlawfully, undermining the entire NDA and the confidentiality clause in Section 2, because there is a breach, and therefore it becomes public information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient ag rees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential and to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information with the same degree of care with which it protects the confidentiality of its own confidential information, but i n no event with less than a reasonable degree of care. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Recipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purp ose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations by stating that information is not considered confidential if it enters the public domain, even due to the recipient's breach, or if received from a third party without the right to disclose. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the recipient to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care and only disclose it to those with appropriate written agreements. The change allows the Recipient to be negligent or complicit in a breach and not be held accountable, thus creating a strong and direct conflict with the obligation to maintain confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, or in the event that a breach appears to be imminent, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to all legal and equitable remedies afforded i t by law, and in addition may recover all reasonable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in seeking such remedies.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, the Disclosing Party's sole remedy shall be limited to a written warning. No monetary damages or legal action may be pursued.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding remedies for breach. The original text allows for all legal and equitable remedies, including recovering costs and attorneys' fees. The modified text limits the Disclosing Party to only a written warning, removing the ability to seek any other recourse. This contradicts the 'all legal and equitable remedies' portion of the original text and introduces ambiguity, rendering it virtually unenforceable, since there is limited ways the Disclosing Party to be able to enforce, creating uncertainty in enforcement and creating an In-Text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the Recipient shall breach this Agreement, the Disclosing Party's sole remedy shall be limited to a written warning. No monetary damages or legal action may be pursued. If the Confidential Information is sought by any third party, including by way of subpoena or other court process, the Recipient shall inform the Disclosing Party of the request in sufficient time to permit the Disclosing Party to object to and, if necessary, seek court intervention to prevent the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly limits the Disclosing Party's remedy to a written warning and prohibits monetary damages or legal action. The contradicted text states the same restriction. The original text, however, allows for all legal and equitable remedies, which directly conflicts with the changed and contradicted texts. While the changed text and contradicted text are identical, the change from the original text to the changed text caused the contradiction. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is introduced by the modification.
